key_themes: Q1 earnings beat overshadowed by lowered 2026 EPS guidance due to Exact Sciences acquisition dilution
Maintained solid comparable sales growth outlook (6.5-7.5%) with strong MedTech (~13%) and oncology diagnostics momentum
Negative near-term price action and analyst PT reductions despite ongoing Moderate Buy consensus and $122+ average targets
summary: Abbott beat Q1 2026 estimates (EPS $1.15 vs ~$1.14 expected; revenue $11.16B beating by ~$170M) on robust Medical Devices growth, but cut full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $5.38–$5.58 (from prior $5.55–$5.80) primarily due to $0.20/share dilution from its completed ~$23B Exact Sciences deal, plus a weaker respiratory season and Q2 guidance below consensus; shares fell 5–7% on the news and have pulled back materially from recent highs.[[1]](https://www.investors.com/news/technology/abbott-laboratories-stock-abbott-laboratories-earnings-q1-2026/)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/abbott-first-quarter-results-beat-cancer-diagnostics-medical-device-strength-2026-04-16/)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABT/) While the acquisition accelerates leadership in high-growth cancer diagnostics (adding meaningful sales) and analysts largely reaffirm Buy/Outperform ratings with average targets around $122–$133 (25–35%+ upside from ~$97), the self-downgrade in profitability created clear near-term disappointment. The biggest risk is sustained margin compression, integration hiccups from the large deal, or continued competitive pressure in diabetes CGM and nutrition.[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/abbott-first-quarter-results-beat-cancer-diagnostics-medical-device-strength-2026-04-16/)[[4]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/abbott-laboratories-abt-buy-rating-reaffirmed-at-btig-research-2026-04-17/)
